column1: Ketel Marte leads the slate with a strong platoon advantage against Michael McGreevy and a favorable park boost, making him a consistent option for a hit at the top of the order. Yandy Diaz offers similar reliability, reaching base in over 72% of games and facing a hittable starter, though without the same matchup edge. Alec Burleson’s recent surge and platoon advantage keep him in play, while Fernando Tatis Jr. and Ernie Clement round out a steady group of alternatives.
